hero.pillBadge: Self-hosted Git platform powered by Gitea
hero.subtitle: A modern UI and powerful CI/CD on top of your own Gitea instance. Host repositories, manage pull requests, track issues, and deploy previews — all on your infrastructure.

features.heading: Features
features.subtitle: Everything you need to host, review, and ship code on your own infrastructure.

gitHosting.title: Git Hosting
gitHosting.description: Host repositories with full Git support, branch protection, deploy keys, and webhooks — powered by Gitea.

pullRequests.title: Pull Requests
pullRequests.description: Create, review, and merge pull requests. Close and reopen PRs with a clean, fast interface.

issueTracking.title: Issue Tracking
issueTracking.description: Track bugs and feature requests. Create, close, and reopen issues with full markdown support.

releaseManagement.title: Release Management
releaseManagement.description: Publish releases with release notes, track versions, and manage your software delivery pipeline.

cicdPipeline.title: CI/CD Pipeline
cicdPipeline.description: Trigger builds, track deployments, and manage your CI/CD workflow with our integrated build system.

teamManagement.title: Team Management
teamManagement.description: Create organizations, manage teams, and control access with fine-grained permissions.

howItWorks.heading: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps to a better Git workflow on your own infrastructure.

step1.title: Connect Gitea
step1.description: Link your self-hosted Gitea instance in seconds. Forge git sits on top of Gitea, giving you a modern UI and powerful CI/CD without migrating your repos.

step2.title: Create & Collaborate
step2.description: Create repositories, manage organizations and teams, review pull requests, and track issues — all from a clean, fast interface your team will love.

step3.title: Build & Deploy
step3.description: Trigger builds, monitor CI/CD pipelines, and deploy preview environments automatically. Ship faster with integrated build tooling.

pricing.heading: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Self-hosted on your infrastructure. No hidden fees, no surprise bills.

free.name: Free
free.description: Self-hosted with community support. Perfect for individuals and small teams.

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: Advanced features for growing teams shipping at speed.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.description: Dedicated infrastructure and support for large organizations.

newsletter.heading: Stay in the loop
newsletter.description: Get product updates, tips for self-hosted Git, and early access to new features.

cta.heading: Ready to ship faster?
cta.description: Self-host your Git workflow with the tools your team needs — pull requests, issues, CI/CD, and more.

footer.brandDescription: Self-hosted Git platform powered by Gitea. Host repositories, manage pull requests, and deploy previews — all on your infrastructure.

login.tagline: Self-hosted Git forge CI/CD. One click from your Gitea instance.
login.headline: Welcome back
login.subhead: Sign in to your forge-git account
login.oauthHint: One-click sign in. Requires OAuth2 configured in your Gitea instance.
login.orUsePat: Or use a personal access token
login.tokenHint: Generate one at your Gitea user settings → Applications
login.urlInvalid: Use a full URL starting with http:// or https://
login.setupHelpStep1: Go to your Gitea instance → Site Administration → Applications → OAuth2 Applications
login.setupHelpStep2: Register a new application with redirect URI:
login.setupHelpStep3: Set the environment variables GITEA_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and GITEA_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET
login.newHereCreateAccount: New here? Create a Gitea account →

oauthError.no-gitea-url: Gitea URL is not configured. Set GITEA_URL environment variable.
oauthError.oauth-not-configured: OAuth is not configured. Set GITEA_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and GITEA_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET.
oauthError.oauth-session-expired: Your OAuth session expired. Please try signing in again.
oauthError.oauth-state-mismatch: Security check failed. Please try signing in again.
oauthError.oauth-exchange-failed: Failed to exchange authorization code. Please try again.
oauthError.oauth-token-invalid: Received an invalid token from Gitea. Please try again.
oauthError.oauth-missing-params: Missing required OAuth parameters. Please try again.
oauthError.oauth-invalid-state: Invalid OAuth state. Please try signing in again.

signup.headline: Don't have an account yet?
signup.subhead: forge-git uses your Gitea instance for everything — repos, auth, identity. You'll need a Gitea account to sign in.
signup.step1PublicTitle: Sign up at a public instance
signup.step1PublicDesc: Use a hosted Gitea service. Free, no setup.
signup.step1SelfHostDesc: Run your own instance. Full control.
signup.step1TeamDesc: Most teams have a shared Gitea instance.
signup.backToSignIn: Already have an account? Sign in →

forgotToken.headline: Forgot your Gitea URL?
forgotToken.subhead: The address of the Gitea instance you sign in to. Often looks like https://git.yourcompany.com
forgotToken.checkBrowserSaved: We saved this from a previous sign-in on this device.
forgotToken.checkBrowserEmpty: Nothing saved on this device. Try the suggestions below or ask your team.
forgotToken.whereToFindEmail: Check the email invite from your team admin
forgotToken.whereToFindTeammate: Ask a teammate for the URL they use
forgotToken.whereToFindHistory: Search your browser history for "gitea" or "forge"
forgotToken.whereToFindWiki: Check your team's wiki or runbook

callback.errorSubhead: Something went wrong completing your Gitea session.
